reforms.description: Denmark's government has implemented a reform allowing universities to claim ownership of their employees' inventions, which enhances their ability to commercialize intellectual property and boost technology transfer. This reform aims to improve technology transfer to enhance the commercialization of research, addressing previous bureaucratic obstacles.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing technology transfer and commercialization is expected to lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into practice, allowing universities to claim ownership of inventions.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly changes the ownership structure of inventions, which is a major shift in policy.
reforms.source_quote: A reform in 2000 improved technology transfer, but research funding is still largely institution-based with little explicit link to quality.

reforms.description: The Danish government is working to simplify complicated tax rules that hinder private investment in innovation and venture capital, while also having introduced a tax credit scheme to encourage collaboration between firms and public research institutions. These measures aim to enhance research partnerships and stimulate private sector investment in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying tax rules and providing tax credits are expected to increase private investment in innovation, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit scheme has been introduced and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms are significant in promoting private investment but do not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: A tax credit scheme was tried out in 2002 whereby a firm received a 150% tax credit for payments made to a public institution as part of a collaborative research project.

reforms.description: Denmark has seen an increase in R&D spending, indicating a commitment to enhancing its research capabilities and innovation environment. The government plans to shift towards a contestable performance-based funding mechanism for research, allowing private firms to compete for public research funds on equal terms, which is expected to improve the effectiveness of the research budget by tying funding to quality outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending and implementing performance-based funding are expected to enhance research quality and relevance, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: R&D spending has increased, and the government is actively working on the funding mechanism.
reforms.importance_rationale: The shift to performance-based funding is significant but does not represent a complete overhaul of the funding system.
reforms.source_quote: Getting better results from the research budget would also be useful. One way is to shift further towards a contestable performance-based funding mechanism and to tie funding to quality.

reforms.description: The Danish government proposed to relax bankruptcy regulations to facilitate easier re-entry for failed entrepreneurs, which is expected to enhance innovation and growth by allowing entrepreneurs to learn from their failures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating re-entry for failed entrepreneurs is expected to foster a more dynamic startup ecosystem and encourage innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal has been made but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant in supporting entrepreneurship but does not represent a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: The government put a proposal to parliament in late 2004 aimed at relaxing these regulations and making it easier for failed entrepreneurs to start over.
